findings.evidence: The declaration is `theorem not_isSquare_prod_primes ... (hinj : Function.Injective p) {S : Finset ι} ...`; the proof only uses distinctness in the call to `Finset.squarefree_prod_of_pairwise_isCoprime`, whose Mathlib signature takes `Set.Pairwise s (IsRelPrime on f)` for the same finset.
findings.fix: Change the helper to assume pairwise distinctness only on `S`, e.g. `Set.Pairwise (S : Set ι) (fun i j => p i ≠ p j)`, and derive that from `hinj` in `finrank_adjoin_sqrt_primes`.
findings.issue: `not_isSquare_prod_primes` proves a fact about one subset `S` but assumes `Function.Injective p` on all of `ι`; primes outside `S` are irrelevant.

summary: The main degree statement is at the expected level, but the public subset-product helper has a globally stronger distinctness hypothesis than its statement needs.
